qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: NRF OAuth token issuance accepts self-asserted NF identity and exposes protected management APIs
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets unauthenticated NF registration and OAuth token minting on `/nnrf-nfm/v1/nf-instances` and `/oauth2/token`, while the advisory targets malformed `group-id-list` input reaching `EncodeGroupId` in the NF discovery path and panicking the service. The root cause, affected code, and consequence differ.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only finalized `qualifying_vuln` finding in this session is an OAuth/NF identity trust bypass. The target advisory is a denial-of-service bug where malformed `group-id-list` input reaches `EncodeGroupId` in the NF discovery code and causes an index-out-of-range panic. No finalized finding identifies that vulnerable parser/validation path or an equivalent root cause.
evidence: The advisory summary and details describe a crafted `GET` request to the NRF discovery service whose malformed `group-id-list` parameter causes `EncodeGroupId` to panic with `index out of range`.

The upstream fix in `.judge/advisory_refs/dac77d8.patch` adds `ValidateGroupIdFormat`, changes `EncodeGroupId`, and updates `internal/sbi/processor/nf_discovery.go`, confirming the advisory root cause is discovery-query input validation around `group-id-list`.

`oauth_token_identity_bypass/vulnerability_report.json` instead describes unauthenticated NF registration, `POST /oauth2/token` issuance, and bearer-token authorization on `/nnrf-nfm` and `/nnrf-disc`, with no `EncodeGroupId`, `group-id-list`, or panic/DoS path.
